why_listen.en: A hypnotic Cuban melody that transports you to the streets of Havana. The gentle interplay of guitar and vocals is pure warmth and nostalgia.
The song that brought Mexican folk music to the world. Its infectious energy and driving rhythm make it impossible not to dance and smile.
Selena's voice is pure joy made audible. This cumbia classic radiates warmth and will have you swaying from the first note.
The father of Afrobeat at his most powerful. This 12-minute groove is a masterclass in rhythm, protest, and the unifying power of music.
Mama Africa's most beloved song is pure sunshine in musical form. The playful Xhosa lyrics and irresistible rhythm make everyone a dancer.
The first African single to sell over a million copies in Europe. Its kora-driven melody and euphoric energy bridge continents and generations.
Nusrat's voice is a force of nature that transcends language. This collaboration with Michael Brook merges Qawwali devotion with modern production into something otherworldly and uplifting.
The only Japanese-language song to reach number one in the US. Its bittersweet melody is universally beautiful and proves music needs no translation.
Pure unfiltered joy and one of the first viral music videos globally. Its Bhangra beat and Daler Mehndi's infectious energy are guaranteed to lift any mood.
Perhaps the most romantic song ever written. Piaf's voice turns the simple idea of seeing the world through rose-colored glasses into something transcendent and eternal.
The song that made the world fall in love with Italian music. Its soaring chorus about flying through a painted blue sky is the definition of joyful abandon.
Louis Armstrong's gravelly voice singing about the beauty of everyday life is the ultimate antidote to cynicism. A song that makes you want to slow down and appreciate being alive.
Every little thing is gonna be alright. Bob Marley distilled the entire philosophy of optimism into one perfect, breezy reggae tune that has comforted millions.
Warda's voice is velvet and fire at once. This classic Arabic love song builds with orchestral grandeur into an overwhelming wave of emotion and beauty.
Fairuz's crystalline voice is the sound of Lebanon and the entire Arab world waking up in the morning. This song is gentle, hopeful, and profoundly beautiful.
